title: tabernaemontana capuronii

These constituents include voacangine, voacristine, and voacarin, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, helping to improve symptoms of conditions such as arthritis, acne, and age-related cognitive decline. parts: Tabernaemontana capuronii has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, leaves, and bark, which are used to treat various health conditions. The roots of Tabernaemontana capuronii contain alkaloids, including voacangine and voacristine, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The leaves of the plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which have been reported to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The bark of Tabernaemontana capuronii has been found to contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including glycosides and phenolic acids, which have been used to treat fever and other infectious diseases.
preparations: Tabernaemontana capuronii has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments. side_effects: Tabernaemontana capuronii can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to its toxic compounds. Ingesting excessive amounts of the plant can lead to severe vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain. Long-term misuse of Tabernaemontana capuronii may also result in liver damage and kidney failure.
